revision.comment: Bot: sync with enwiki
revision.text:
{{pp|small=yes}} {{redirect|Template:Inline list|a navbox listing templates used within lines of text to mark problems needing attention|Template:Inline cleanup tags}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{used in system|in [[MediaWiki:Pageinfo-header]]}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{tlr|startflatlist|flt}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Hlist/styles.css}} This template provides a [[Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|WCAG]]/ISO-standards-compliant accessible alternative to comma- or dash- (or other single character-) separated lists, per [[WP:FLATLIST]] and [[MOS:HLIST]]. Lists output by {{tl|Flatlist}} and {{tl|Hlist}} are semantically and visually identical, and differ only in the wiki-markup used to create the lists. {{TOC limit|3}} == Usage == {{tl|flatlist}} starts a horizontal list, such as: {{flatlist| * [[cat]] * [[dog]] * [[horse]] * [[cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} If this template is used with no parameters, {{tl|endflatlist}} is required. For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[cat]] * [[dog]] * [[horse]] * [[cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[cat]] * [[dog]] * [[horse]] * [[cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[cat]] * [[dog]] * [[horse]] * [[cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[cat]] * [[dog]] * [[horse]] * [[cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
  • cat
  • dog
  • horse
  • cow
  • sheep
  • pig
 |} == See also == * {{tl|endflatlist}} * {{tl|hlist}} * {{tl|plainlist}} and {{tl|unbulleted list}} * {{tl|bulleted list}} * {{tl|ordered list}} * {{tl|nowrap}} * {{tl|pagelist}} * [[WP:HLIST]] {{HTML lists}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Typing-aid templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates|Flatlist]] }}

For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
  • cat
  • dog
  • horse
  • cow
  • sheep
  • pig
 |} == See also == * {{tl|endflatlist}} * {{tl|hlist}} * {{tl|plainlist}} and {{tl|unbulleted list}} * {{tl|bulleted list}} * {{tl|ordered list}} * {{tl|nowrap}} * {{tl|pagelist}} * [[WP:HLIST]] {{HTML lists}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Typing-aid templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates|Flatlist]] }}

For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |

{{pp|small=yes}} {{redirect|Template:Inline list|a navbox listing templates used within lines of text to mark problems needing attention|Template:Inline cleanup tags}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{used in system|in [[MediaWiki:Pageinfo-header]]}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{tlr|startflatlist|flt}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Hlist/styles.css}} This template provides a [[Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|WCAG]]/ISO-standards-compliant accessible alternative to comma- or dash- (or other single character-) separated lists, per [[WP:FLATLIST]] and [[MOS:HLIST]]. Lists output by {{tl|Flatlist}} and {{tl|Hlist}} are semantically and visually identical, and differ only in the wiki-markup used to create the lists. {{TOC limit|3}} == Usage == {{tl|flatlist}} starts a horizontal list, such as: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} If this template is used with no parameters, {{tl|endflatlist}} is required. For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[first]] # [[second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[fourth]] # [[fifth]] # [[sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
  • cat
  • dog
  • horse
  • cow
  • sheep
  • pig
 |} == See also == * {{tl|endflatlist}} * {{tl|hlist}} * {{tl|plainlist}} and {{tl|unbulleted list}} * {{tl|bulleted list}} * {{tl|ordered list}} * {{tl|nowrap}} * {{tl|pagelist}} * [[WP:HLIST]] {{HTML lists}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Typing-aid templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates|Flatlist]] }}

background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! {{pp|small=yes}} {{redirect|Template:Inline list|a navbox listing templates used within lines of text to mark problems needing attention|Template:Inline cleanup tags}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{used in system|in [[MediaWiki:Pageinfo-header]]}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{tlr|startflatlist|flt}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Hlist/styles.css}} This template provides a [[Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|WCAG]]/ISO-standards-compliant accessible alternative to comma- or dash- (or other single character-) separated lists, per [[WP:FLATLIST]] and [[MOS:HLIST]]. Lists output by {{tl|Flatlist}} and {{tl|Hlist}} are semantically and visually identical, and differ only in the wiki-markup used to create the lists. {{TOC limit|3}} == Usage == {{tl|flatlist}} starts a horizontal list, such as: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[pig]] }} If this template is used with no parameters, {{tl|endflatlist}} is required. For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. Lists output by {{tl|Flatlist}} and {{tl|Hlist}} are semantically and visually identical, and differ only in the wiki-markup used to create the lists. {{TOC limit|3}} == Usage == {{tl|flatlist}} starts a horizontal list, such as: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} If this template is used with no parameters, {{tl|endflatlist}} is required. For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
  • cat
  • dog
  • horse
  • cow
  • sheep
  • pig
 |} == See also == * {{tl|endflatlist}} * {{tl|hlist}} * {{tl|plainlist}} and {{tl|unbulleted list}} * {{tl|bulleted list}} * {{tl|ordered list}} * {{tl|nowrap}} * {{tl|pagelist}} * [[WP:HLIST]] {{HTML lists}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Typing-aid templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates|Flatlist]] }}

Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |

(Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
  • cat
  • dog
  • horse
  • cow
  • sheep
  • pig
 |} == See also == * {{tl|endflatlist}} * {{tl|hlist}} * {{tl|plainlist}} and {{tl|unbulleted list}} * {{tl|bulleted list}} * {{tl|ordered list}} * {{tl|nowrap}} * {{tl|pagelist}} * [[WP:HLIST]] {{HTML lists}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Typing-aid templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:List formatting and function templates|Flatlist]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{pp|small=yes}} {{redirect|Template:Inline list|a navbox listing templates used within lines of text to mark problems needing attention|Template:Inline cleanup tags}} {{Documentation subpage}} {{used in system|in [[MediaWiki:Pageinfo-header]]}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{tlr|startflatlist|flt}} {{Uses TemplateStyles|Template:Hlist/styles.css}} This template provides a [[:en:Web Content Accessibility Guidelines|WCAG]]/ISO-standards-compliant accessible alternative to comma- or dash- (or other single character-) separated lists, per [[WP:FLATLIST]] and [[:en:MOS:HLIST|MOS:HLIST]]. Lists output by {{tl|Flatlist}} and {{tl|Hlist}} are semantically and visually identical, and differ only in the wiki-markup used to create the lists. {{TOC limit|3}} == Usage == {{tl|flatlist}} starts a horizontal list, such as: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} If this template is used with no parameters, {{tl|endflatlist}} is required. For navigation boxes using {{tl|navbox}}, one can set {{para|listclass|hlist}}, and achieve the same styling without using this template. For image captions, list (i.e. asterisk) markup does not work, so consider using {{tl|hlist}} instead. == Parameters == {{#switch:{{NAMESPACE}}:{{ROOTPAGENAME}}|#default= {{Format TemplateData| { "description": { "en": "Used to create plain (i.e. unbulleted) lists." }, "format": "inline", "params": { "1": { "label": { "en": "Content" }, "description": { "en": "The list to wrap." }, "suggested": true, "type": "content" }, "class": { "label": { "en": "Extra Classes" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS classes to the containing div." }, "type": "string" }, "style": { "label": { "en": "CSS Style" }, "description": { "en": "Adds extra CSS properties to the containing div. Complex styles should not be used in articles (per [[MOS:DEVIATIONS]]) but may be acceptable on user, project and talk pages." }, "example": "\n border: solid 1px silver;\n background: lightyellow;\n", "type": "string" }, "indent": { "label": { "en": "Indent" }, "description": { "en": "Indents the list by a number of standard indents (one indent being 1.6[[em (typography)|em]]). (Useful in an indented discussion thread.)" }, "example": "2", "type": "number" } }, "paramOrder": [ "class", "style", "indent", "1" ] } }} |Template:Endflatlist= {{Format TemplateData|JSON= { "description": "Ends a flat list created by {{tl|flatlist}}.", "format": "inline", "params": { } } }} }} == Examples == {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} produces: {{flatlist| * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] }} === Alternative syntax === {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} produces: {{startflatlist}} * [[:en:Cat|Cat]] * [[:en:Dog|Dog]] * [[:en:Horse|Horse]] * [[:en:Cow|Cow]] * [[:en:Sheep|Sheep]] * [[:en:Pig|Pig]] {{endflatlist}} === Syntax for ordered lists === {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} produces: {{flatlist| # [[:en:First|First]] # [[:en:Second|Second]] # [[:en:3|third]] # [[:en:Fourth|Fourth]] # [[:en:Fifth|Fifth]] # [[:en:Sixth|Sixth]] }} === Using optional parameters === {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} produces: {{flatlist |indent=2 |style=border:solid 1px silver; background:lightyellow; |class=nowraplinks | * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1936 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1948 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1952 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1956 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1960 Winter Olympics]] * [[:en:Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics|Alpine skiing at the 1964 Winter Olympics]] }} == Technical details == This template uses the .hlist [[:en:Cascading Style Sheets|CSS]] class defined in [[MediaWiki:Common.css]] to generate horizontal lists. It causes ordinary html list items to be displayed inline (horizontally), where they would normally display as block elements (vertically). The class also generates the [[:en:Interpunct|Interpunct]]s between list items and parentheses around nested lists. {| class="wikitable" ! Wikitext ! Expanded template ! HTML |- style="vertical-align:top;" | {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} | {{nowiki2|tag=pre| {{flatlist| * cat * dog * horse * cow * sheep * pig }} }} |
